name: Abkhazia (Georgia)

colors.symbolism: Islam and the alternating broad horizontal stripes
Christianity, the alternating stripes, and the seven stars and the open hand
the canton — recalling the medieval Kingdom of Abkhazia

description: Seven horizontal stripes alternating green and white, with a red rectangular canton at the upper hoist bearing a white open right hand and seven white five-pointed stars in an arc above it.
history: Adopted on 23 July 1992 by the Supreme Council of the de-facto Abkhaz Republic. The seven stripes and seven stars represent the seven historical regions of Abkhazia. Abkhazia is internationally recognised as a Georgian autonomous republic but has been administered as a de-facto independent state since the 1992–1993 war; the flag is used in this de-facto capacity.

features.symbolism: the seven historical regions of Abkhazia
an open right hand — the Abkhaz greeting and the medieval kingdom's emblem
